role: President-elect of NCRI (1993–present); co-leader of MEK
bio: Maryam Rajavi studied metallurgy at Sharif University of Technology and joined the [Mojahedin-e Khalq](/orgs/mojahedin-e-khalq) after two of her sisters were killed — one by SAVAK and one executed by [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini)'s regime in 1982. She rose through MEK ranks, serving as joint leader (1985–1989) and Secretary General (1989–1993), before being elected President-elect of the [National Council of Resistance of Iran](/orgs/national-council-of-resistance-of-iran) in 1993. She married [Massoud Rajavi](/people/massoud-rajavi) in 1985. Under her leadership, women hold over half of senior positions in the organization. She is based in exile between France and Albania.
editorial_notes: Previously married to Mehdi Abrishamchi (1980-1985). Briefly arrested in 2003 French raid on NCRI offices.
